resources.string: Defend the meadows of Egril against the Hordes of Gleid Thyae under the Banner of the evil Ewhad
Are you sure you want to exit the game?
The enemies have breached your defenses...
This will backup your current game data and restore the original levels from the repository. Your current data will be preserved in a backup folder. Are you sure?
Game data has been restored from the repository. Your previous data was backed up to: %s
Click the settings icon to customize your experience. Available settings include:
Add an optional comment to help identify this save:
Download all save files as a ZIP archive
A save file named "%s" already exists. What would you like to do?
Failed to import some files. Please check the file format.
Defender of Egril is a turn-based tower defense game. Defend the meadows of Egril against waves of enemies under the evil banner of Ewhad.
At the start of each level:
Towers are ready to attack immediately
Place New Towers - costs coins, requires build time
Upgrade Existing Towers - increases damage and range
Attack with Towers - select tower, then target
End Your Turn - enemies move and attack
Enemies that reach the end cost you 1 health
Win: Eliminate all enemies before they breach your defenses
Spike Tower (Pike): Melee defense, cheapest option
Spear Tower: Medium range piercing attacks
Ballista Tower: Long range siege, minimum range
Green Witch: Can heal other enemies
Focus fire on tough enemies (Ogres, Orks)
Wizard Towers for massive Areal damage
Upgrade high-level towers rather than building new ones
Attack Enemies - click tower with actions, then enemy in range
Fireball (Area): Damages all enemies within 1 cell of impact
Acid: Continuous damage for 3 turns within 1 cell of impact
Victory: Defeat all enemies in all waves with at least 1 HP remaining

Defeat: Weak enemies (goblins, orks, ogres, skeletons) cost 1 HP when reaching the target.
Mighty enemies (wizards, witches, mages, demons, dragons) cost HP equal to their level.
Ewhad costs all your HP.
At 0 HP, you lose.
Special: dig for valuables or place traps on the path
Do you really want to sell the %s for %s %s?
The dragon\'s lair... a reminder of greed\'s consequences.
Initial Building Phase - Place towers (no build time)
Add an optional comment to help identify this save:
Your game has been saved successfully!
Level and actions shown on towers
Your Turn - Place towers and attack enemies
Click or drag to paint hexagons. Use Ctrl+Scroll to zoom:
This will replace all NO_PLAY tiles with PATH tiles. Are you sure?
Ewhad can only be spawned once per level!
Are you sure you want to remove "%s" from the level sequence? The level will not be deleted and can be added back later.
Cannot delete this turn while later turns exist
No enemies spawning in this turn
Are you sure you want to remove all spawn turns?
Are you sure you want to delete this level?
This turn contains units with different levels (%s). Are you sure you want to change all of them to level %d?
This is a tower defense game. Your goal is to stop enemies from reaching the target by building defensive towers.

You can move around the map using two-finger swipe gestures or by tapping the navigation cross (arrows) on the screen.

You can also click on the minimap to quickly jump to different areas of the battlefield.

Use pinch gestures to zoom in and out for a better view.

You can move around the map using WASD keys, arrow keys, or by dragging with the mouse.

You can also click on the minimap to quickly jump to different areas of the battlefield.

Use the mouse wheel to zoom in and out for a better view.

You have coins to build towers and health points.
When you defeat enemies, you earn coins equal to their base reward multiplied by their level.
Weak enemies cost 1 HP, but mighty enemies (wizards, witches, mages, demons, dragons) cost HP equal to their level.
Ewhad costs all your HP!
Spike Tower: Close range, cheap. Spear Tower: Medium range. Bow Tower: Long range. Choose wisely based on your strategy!
The Legend on the right shows you what different colors and symbols mean on the game map. It explains build areas (green), enemy paths (brown), and special tiles.
The Enemy List shows all enemies currently on the map and those that will spawn in future turns. Use this to plan your defenses! You can see each enemy's health, level, and when they will arrive.
Select a tower type from below, then click on a green build area to place it. Build your defenses!
In this initial phase, towers are built instantly but cannot attack yet.\nClick on a tower to see its range - path tiles get green borders.\nLater in the game, towers will take 1-2 turns to build.
During this initial phase, you can undo tower placement by selecting it and selling for a full refund. After the battle starts, you can only sell towers for 75%% of their value.
Click the Start Battle button when ready. Your goal is to defend the Target at the end of the path - don't let enemies reach it!
Goblins are fast but weak. The Ork is slow but tough. Position your towers wisely!
Select a tower to see which enemies are in range (highlighted). Towers can only attack enemies within their range!

Click on an enemy in range to attack.\nEach tower has limited actions per turn shown by lightning symbols.\nIf your tower can't reach enemies or has no actions left, you can end your turn or place more towers.

Select a tower and click Upgrade to increase its stats. You'll see current damage, range, and actions per turn, plus what they'll be after upgrading. Upgrades cost coins but make towers more powerful!
Select a tower and click Sell to get back 75%% of its total investment (cost + upgrades). Use this if you need coins or want to reposition your defense.
You can save your game at any time using the Save Game button.\nThis lets you try different strategies or continue later.\nTo load a saved game, use the Load Game button on the world map screen.

You've mastered the basics! In later levels, you'll unlock mightier towers like the Ballista, Wizard, and Alchemy towers to face stronger foes like demons and bosses. Now defend Egril against the hordes. Good luck, defender!
Double coins and health, towers start at level 3
Enemies spawn 3 times with increasing levels
Note: Changing difficulty does not affect the current level. Start or restart a level to apply.
The dragon alternates movement:\nTurn 1, 3, 5...: Walks 1 tile on path.\nTurn 2, 4, 6...: Flies up to 5 tiles, can cross obstacles but must land on path
Dragons devour enemies they land on (except Ewhad), gaining their health points!
Dragon level increases with health: Level = HP ÷ 500 (min. 1)
The dragon has grown greedy! Greed = Dragon Level ÷ 5. The dragon will now devour units on adjacent tiles equal to its greed level each turn.

The dragon is very greedy (greed > 5)! It will now hunt down and destroy dwarven mines, absorbing all the health a new dragon would have (500 HP per mine). Mines will become destroyed rubble. Defend them or rebuild quickly!
The dragon approaches! The dwarves prepare to defend their mine, but it may not be enough...
You have only 1 health point! Not a single enemy can reach the target, or you will lose. Defend with extreme caution!
Eats surrounding enemy units and destroys mines one after another
Mighty unit! Deals %d HP damage if it reaches the target (level × 1 HP)
BOSS! If Ewhad reaches the target, the level is lost!
Configure waypoint connections for this level. Waypoints define the path enemies will follow.
No waypoints configured for this level.
Circular dependency detected! Fix waypoint connections.
Select the spawn point for this enemy
No spawn points available on this map
Some enemies are spawned at positions that are not spawn points on the current map. Use this dialog to remap them to valid spawn points.
Some enemies are spawned outside valid spawn points
A waypoint connection from this position already exists
Are you sure you want to remove all waypoint connections?
Click on waypoints on the map to connect them
New maps and levels have been found in the repository that are not in your game data. Would you like to add them?
The level sequence will be updated.
Note: If you had changed the sequence file, you will need to redo those changes. Your current sequence will be backed up to sequence-N.json.
You have unsaved progress in this level. What would you like to do?
You have towers with unused action points and enemies in range, or mines with unused actions. Are you sure you want to end your turn?
Note: After auto-attack completes, if mines or towers with trap abilities or lasting attacks still have action points, you can use them before the turn ends.
Auto-attack completed, but the following towers still have actions that cannot be automated effectively:
Mines: Can dig for coins or build traps
Alchemy Towers: Lasting attacks benefit from manual targeting
Wizard Towers: Can place magical traps (level 10+)
Are you sure you want to remove this barricade?

Your Spear tower has reached level 10 (or Spike tower level 20) and can now build barricades!
Barricades are wooden structures placed on adjacent path tiles that block enemy movement.
Spear tower barricades have HP = level - 10 (minimum 1). Spike tower barricades have HP = (level - 20) / 2 (minimum 1).
When enemies reach a barricade, they attack it with damage equal to their level (dragons deal 5× their level).
If a barricade is destroyed, the enemy moves to that tile.
You can build new barricades or reinforce existing ones, and click on barricades to remove them.

Your wizard tower has reached level 10 and can now place magical traps! These traps teleport enemies back to their spawn point when triggered. After placing a trap, there is a 10-turn cooldown before you can place another one.
Are you sure you want to remove this magical trap?
Are you sure you want to remove this trap?
The Final Stand is not connected to any entry point
Select levels that must be completed before this level is unlocked:
Leave empty to require all prerequisites. Enter a number to require only that many.
Select a map from the list, then click on the world map to set its position. The position is saved as a permille (0-1000) of the map dimensions.

Your tower has reached level 20 and now has an extended area attack! Fireball and acid attacks now affect enemies within 2 tiles of the target instead of just 1 tile. This makes your area attacks much more powerful against groups of enemies!

The Wizard Tower is a powerful area-of-effect tower that launches fireballs at enemies!
Each fireball damages the target and all enemies in adjacent tiles.
It has a longer build time (2 turns) but can devastate groups of enemies.
At level 10, wizards can place magical traps that teleport enemies back to spawn.
At level 20, the fireball radius increases to 2 tiles!

The Alchemy Tower throws acid that deals damage over time!
Unlike other towers, acid damage persists for multiple rounds (base 2 turns, increases with level).
The acid affects the target and adjacent enemies in a small area.
While the damage per turn is lower, the total damage over time can be very effective.
At level 20, the acid area increases to 2 tiles!

The Ballista is a long-range siege weapon with exceptional range (5 tiles) and high damage!
However, it has a minimum range of 3 tiles and cannot attack nearby enemies.
It requires 2 turns to build.
Use it to target enemies from a distance before they get too close, or position it behind other towers for maximum effectiveness.

The Dwarven Mine is a special structure that generates coins through mining operations!
Unlike combat towers, mines use the "dig" action to find valuable resources (rubble, brass, silver, gold, gems, or diamonds).
Mines can also build traps that damage enemies.
At higher levels, mines can dig more times per turn and reach further.
Warning: Dragons are greedy and will destroy mines to claim the treasure!
You can place towers on river tiles! When you do, they will be placed on rafts.
Rafts move automatically with the river flow at the end of each turn. The flow speed determines how many tiles they move (1 or 2 tiles).
Rafts are destroyed if they reach a maelstrom (whirlpool) or move out of the map boundaries. The tower on the raft is also destroyed.
Bridges block raft movement. Rafts cannot pass through bridges spanning the river. Multiple rafts on the same river move in order - downstream rafts move first.
Dwarven mines cannot be placed on river tiles. Mines require solid ground and cannot be built on rafts. Please select a build area on land instead.
The Green Witch can heal other enemies! At the end of each enemy turn, she heals all adjacent enemies by 5 times her level (e.g., level 2 witch heals 10 HP per turn).
She will move towards Ewhad if he has damaged health to heal him. Focus on defeating the Green Witch quickly or she will make battles much harder!
The Red Witch can disable your towers! She disables the nearest ready tower of her level or lower for 3 turns.
Disabled towers cannot attack and show "Disabled: XT" in their info panel. Upgrade your towers to higher levels or defeat the Red Witch quickly!
Are you sure you want to delete this saved game?
No icon selected (will use numbered marker)
Welcome to the Level Editor! This tool allows you to create custom maps, levels, and level sequences for Defender of Egril.

To add new levels to the game: First, select or create a map in the Map Editor. Then, create a level using that map in the Level Editor. Finally, bind the level on the World Map to make it visible and playable in the game.
You can copy existing maps and levels and adapt them to create new content quickly. This is a great way to learn and iterate on your designs.

The Restore Game Data button in Settings restores the game data from the game\'s own repository (stored locally, not online). When you use this function, your previous version with any user changes will be saved under a new name in the same folder, so your work is preserved.
All editor data is stored in JSON format on your local filesystem:
maps/ - Contains all map definitions - levels/ - Contains all level definitions - sequence.json - Defines level order - worldmap.json - Defines world map positions
The Map Editor lets you create the grid layout for your levels. Maps define the hexagonal grid, spawn points, paths, and build areas where towers can be placed.

Select and place tile types (PATH, BUILD_AREA, ISLAND, NO_PLAY, SPAWN_POINT, TARGET, RIVER) - Paint tiles by clicking or dragging - Configure river properties (flow direction and speed) - Zoom in/out with Ctrl+Scroll or zoom buttons - Collapse the header for more editing space - Change all NO_PLAY tiles to PATH at once
The Level Editor lets you create gameplay levels using existing maps. Each level references a map and defines the enemies, resources, and available towers.

Select a map for your level - Set starting coins and health points - Configure enemy spawns by turn - Choose which tower types are available - Add spawn points and waypoints - Configure tower restrictions and special rules
The Level Sequence tab lets you arrange levels in the order they appear in the game. This defines the progression and difficulty curve.
Drag and drop levels to reorder - Use ↑ and ↓ buttons to move levels - Add levels to the sequence - Remove levels from the sequence - Set level dependencies
The World Map Positions tab lets you define where levels appear on the world map and how they are connected.

Set X/Y coordinates for each level - Create connections between levels (road or sea) - Auto-connect levels based on dependencies - Configure connection visual styles - Define waypoints for connection paths

Save frequently to avoid losing work - Test your levels in the game to ensure they are balanced - Use the existing levels as templates - Maps can be reused across multiple levels - The sequence determines which levels are playable
Open Source Turn Based Fantasy Tower Defense Game
Enter a new name for this player
Player name is too long (max 50 characters)
A player with this name already exists
No player profiles found. Create a new one to start playing!
Are you sure you want to delete player "%s"? All progress and save files for this player will be permanently deleted.
Critical game data files are missing or empty. The game cannot start without these files.
Please reinstall the game or restore the game data from the Settings menu.
Include player progress (won/unlocked levels) when downloading saves
Download only your level progress without save data
The world map progress in this save file differs from your current progress. Which version would you like to keep?

You can now use the Auto-Attack function. When you select "End Turn" and there are usable action points left, the dialog gives the option "Auto-Attack and End Turn". Then, towers with action points left automatically attack enemies. Targets enemies by priority (bosses first, then special enemies). If there are actions left (mines, traps, acid attacks with no enemy in range), you get a message stating which kind of action is still usable, otherwise the turn ends.
Select your preferred language for the game
How to Install Defender of Egril
1. Download the installer (.exe or .msi) from GitHub Releases
3. If Windows Defender SmartScreen appears with a warning:
This warning appears because the game is not signed with a commercial certificate. The game is safe to install.
• Go to Settings → Apps → Special app access → Install unknown apps
• Select your browser and enable "Allow from this source"
2. Download the APK from GitHub Releases
For security, disable "Install unknown apps" after installation.
2. Or build from source using Xcode
Just visit the game URL in any modern browser (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge)
For detailed instructions, see INSTALL.md in the GitHub repository.
No achievements earned yet. Play the game to earn achievements!
Upgrade a tower to the next level
Build your first raft on a river
Upgrade a raft tower to the next level
Lose a raft when it flows off the map edge
Lose a raft when it sinks in a maelstrom
Win a level without losing any health points
Win a level with only one health point remaining
Kill at least 2 enemies in the same turn
Kill at least 2 enemies with a single attack
Dig in a dwarven mine for the first time
Find gold while digging in a mine
Find a diamond while digging in a mine
Summon a dragon by digging too deep
Build at least 10 towers in a single level
Build at least 10 rafts in a single level
Add health points to an existing barricade
This is an official map. You can copy it to create a user version.
This is an official level. You can copy it to create a user version.

You have made changes to official game data (maps or levels).

These changes will be overwritten when you restart the game, as official content is restored from the repository on each launch.

To preserve your changes, consider copying the official content to create user versions instead.
You have saved an official level.\n\nThese changes will be overwritten when you restart the game, as official content is restored from the repository on each launch.
Official levels can only use official maps.\n\nTo use this map, please copy it to create an official version, or create a user level instead.
100% copyright free, no attribution required. Cannot be re-sold as audio, must be used in context of a project.
Royalty-free music from Fesliyan Studios. Video game use permitted.
Pixabay Content License - Free to use, no attribution required
All audio files are used under their respective licenses. Attribution is provided where required.
